body_txt: This trope is meant to cover a character that decides to use their time in jail by learning, even reading if they Never Learned to Read . Along the way, they may even pick up other skills, such as mechanics, or maybe even get a high school/university diploma equivalent. Usually this trope ends with the character being set on the path of good, however, just because someone decides to hit the books, that doesn't mean they are going to abandon a life of crime. They can certainly use what they've learned and apply it to crime. A lowly purse snatcher can learn how to pull off six-figure scams using knowledge obtained while studying. Be very careful around these characters, as while they may not reach the heights of the Magnificent Bastard or the Evil Genius , they still took the time they were incarcerated to be Dumbass No More . Very much Truth in Television , as several countries offer programs designed to rehabilitate prisoners that tend to involve various forms of education, ranging from high school/university diplomas to vocational education. If the library is not up to snuff, then a Correspondence Course is usually involved, since inmates can't (usually) leave the prison, although learning from other inmates, especially from The Old Convict , is also an option. For other things inmates might do in jail, see Had to Come to Prison to Be a Crook , for when they use it to become better criminals and Prisons Are Gymnasiums , when the prisoners decide to put on muscle while on the inside. Compare with Boxed Crook , in which a prisoner is recruited due to their skills in exchange for a reduced sentence or an outright release and Trading Bars for Stripes , which has the would-be jailbird being given the opportunity to avoid jail altogether in exchange for military service.
